briefing.headline: Vertical and precise — post-spawn muddy conditions call for reaction baits on deep structure.
briefing.gameplan:
In post-spawn with muddy water and pre frontal conditions, reaction presentations shine.

Wheeler: Aggressively snaps the Jigging Rap 4-5 feet above grouped-up bass to force committed fish to swim up; when they reach the bait and you stop it, it glides naturally downward and that's when you get bit.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Match the conditions — aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

Hackney: 'In the South, shad spawn on hard bottoms, matted vegetation, cypress trees, and boat docks. Old Styrofoam marina docks are the best. On the Tennessee River, the better shad spawn is on shell beds and shallow bars in 5-6 feet of water.'

KVD: 'Shad spawn happens around 72 degrees, after bass/crappie spawn — Mother Nature helping them recover by providing a feast. Spikes on full moon, lasts 2-4 weeks. Starts in one area of lake — keep moving until you see activity. Most happens at ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal post-spawn bass are transitioning and aggressive. Males guard fry in 2-6ft while females roam 5-12ft feeding heavily. Target both shallow fry-guarders and roaming post-spawn females near points.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
